normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem canonicality_precond {denoms : Denoms} {amount : Nat} (hcan : Canonical denoms) : Canonical denoms := hcan
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume the denomination system is canonical (greedy is optimal).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem alignment_length (denoms : Denoms) (amount : Nat) : (coinChangeCanonical denoms amount).length = denoms.length
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned counts vector has the same length as the denominations.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem decomposition_correct (denoms : Denoms) (amount : Nat) : Decomposes denoms (coinChangeCanonical denoms amount) amount
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned counts decompose the target amount via the weighted sum.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem counts_nonneg (denoms : Denoms) (amount : Nat) : ∀ i, i < (coinChangeCanonical denoms amount).length → 0 ≤ (coinChangeCanonical denoms amount).get ⟨i, by simpa using ‹i < (coinChangeCanonical denoms amount).length›⟩

theorem optimality_min_sum (denoms : Denoms) (amount : Nat) (hcan : Canonical denoms) : ∀ counts : Counts, Decomposes denoms counts amount → (List.sum counts) ≤ (List.sum (coinChangeCanonical denoms amount))
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Among all valid decompositions, the returned counts minimize the total number of coins.
